name: SQL Injection
description: A vulnerability that allows attackers to interfere with database queries.
mitigation: Use parameterized queries, prepared statements, and input validation.

name: Cross-Site Scripting
description: A vulnerability that allows attackers to inject malicious JavaScript into web pages.
mitigation: Use output encoding, input validation, and Content Security Policy.

name: Server-Side Request Forgery
description: A vulnerability where the server makes unintended requests on behalf of the attacker.
mitigation: Restrict outbound requests, validate destinations, and use allowlists.

name: Remote Code Execution
description: A vulnerability that allows attackers to execute arbitrary code on the server.
mitigation: Validate inputs, patch vulnerable components, and isolate execution environments.

name: Broken Access Control
description: A vulnerability that allows unauthorized users to access restricted resources.
mitigation: Enforce server-side authorization checks and apply least privilege.

name: Prompt Injection
description: A vulnerability where an attacker manipulates an AI model through crafted instructions to bypass intended behavior or safety controls.
mitigation: Use input filtering, instruction separation, output validation, and defense-in-depth prompt design.

name: Indirect Prompt Injection
description: A vulnerability where malicious instructions are hidden in external content that an AI system later consumes and follows.
mitigation: Treat external content as untrusted, isolate instructions from data, and validate model actions before execution.

name: Sensitive Information Disclosure
description: A vulnerability where an AI system unintentionally reveals confidential or sensitive information in its responses.
mitigation: Apply strict data filtering, limit training data exposure, and implement output validation and redaction mechanisms.

name: Insecure Output Handling
description: A vulnerability where AI-generated outputs are used without validation, potentially leading to code execution or injection attacks.
mitigation: Always treat model outputs as untrusted, validate before execution, and avoid directly passing outputs into sensitive systems.

name: Tool Misuse
description: A vulnerability where an AI system misuses connected tools or APIs due to manipulated input or malicious instructions.
mitigation: Implement strict permission controls, validate tool calls, and isolate high-risk operations.

name: Data Exfiltration via LLM
description: A vulnerability where attackers extract sensitive data from an AI system through crafted prompts or repeated queries.
mitigation: Limit context exposure, implement rate limiting, and monitor unusual query patterns.

name: RAG Injection
description: A vulnerability where malicious content is injected into retrieved documents, influencing the AI system's response.
mitigation: Validate retrieved content, separate instructions from data, and apply trust boundaries on external sources.
